summary: Teams UX Bug Fix verified. Microsoft Teams integration card is correctly GREYED OUT (disabled) for non-pro accounts in both (1) Settings > Integrations page and (2) Appointment Creation Wizard. Only 'advanced' level (pro) accounts see Teams as active/green.

wizard_tests: Sandrine (standard): Teams option is GREYED OUT with cursor-not-allowed and bg-slate-50/60 styling
Sandrine (standard): Teams option does NOT show 'Automatique' badge
Sandrine (standard): Teams option shows 'Réservé aux comptes Microsoft 365 Pro. Activez Teams avancé dans Paramètres > Intégrations.'
Sandrine (standard): Google Meet is available and selected by default
Clara (advanced): Teams option is ACTIVE with violet styling (border-violet-400 bg-violet-50)
Clara (advanced): Teams option shows 'Réunion Teams créée automatiquement sous votre identité Microsoft'
Clara (advanced): Teams is auto-selected as default provider
Marie (null): Teams option is GREYED OUT with cursor-not-allowed and bg-slate-50/60 styling
Marie (null): Teams option does NOT show 'Automatique' badge
Marie (null): Teams option shows 'Connectez votre calendrier Outlook pour activer Microsoft Teams.'
integrations_page_tests: Sandrine (standard): Teams calendar note is GREY (bg-slate-50 border-slate-200)
Sandrine (standard): Teams note shows 'Les liens Teams ne peuvent pas être créés automatiquement pour vos RDV visio Microsoft. Vous avez besoin d'un compte Microsoft 365 Pro pour ceci.'
Clara (advanced): Teams calendar note is GREEN (bg-emerald-50/50 border-emerald-100)
Clara (advanced): Teams note shows 'Création de liens Teams active' and 'Récupération de présences disponible'
Marie (null): Teams calendar note is GREY (bg-slate-50 border-slate-200)
Marie (null): Teams note shows 'La création automatique de liens Teams nécessite un compte Microsoft 365 professionnel connecté via Outlook. Connectez un compte Outlook compatible ci-dessus.'

context_for_next_testing_agent: Teams UX Bug Fix verified. Key implementation details: (1) In AppointmentWizard.js lines 624-659, Teams provider option uses canUse = p?.level === 'advanced' to determine if clickable. Non-advanced users see greyed out option with cursor-not-allowed styling. (2) In Integrations.js lines 698-740, Teams calendar note uses isActive = teamsLevel === 'advanced' to determine green vs grey styling. (3) Three user levels: 'advanced' (pro account with has_online_meetings_scope), 'standard' (Outlook connected but no pro scope), null (no Outlook connection). Only 'advanced' users can use Teams auto-generation.
screenshots: .screenshots/sandrine_wizard_step2.png - Shows Teams greyed out for standard user
.screenshots/sandrine_integrations_full.png - Shows Teams calendar note grey for standard user
.screenshots/clara_wizard_step2.png - Shows Teams active/selected for advanced user
.screenshots/clara_integrations_full.png - Shows Teams calendar note green for advanced user
.screenshots/marie_wizard_step2.png - Shows Teams greyed out for null user
.screenshots/marie_integrations.png - Shows Teams calendar note grey for null user
